info.name: Maisa Workflows API
info.description: API calls used by the Maisa GitHub Actions workflows (export-workers, import-workers, export-maisa-orchestrator, import-maisa-orchestrator).

variable.description: Maisa Studio API base URL (source or target environment)
Bearer token — value from MAISA_API_KEYS_JSON for the workspace

item.name: Workers
item.description: Endpoints used in export-workers.yml and import-workers.yml

request.description:
Used in export-workers.yml — lists all workers for the authenticated user.

The workflow iterates over the returned list and calls the export endpoint for each worker.

Expected response: array of worker objects, or a paginated object with an 'items' / 'workers' / 'data' array.

Corresponds to: GET /workers/me (operationId: GetMyWorkers)

Used in export-workers.yml — exports a worker definition by ID.

The response bytes are saved as a .b64 file in the Git repository under:
  workers/<workspace_name>/<timestamp>/<name>__<type>__<id>.b64

If the response bytes are valid UTF-8 they are saved as-is; otherwise they are base64-encoded first.

Corresponds to: GET /workers/{workerId}/export (operationId: ExportWorker)

Used in import-workers.yml — imports a worker definition into a specific organization/workspace.

The 'definition' field contains the content read from the .b64 snapshot file in Git.

Workflow inputs required:
  - target_organization_id  → :orgId
  - target_workspace_id     → :wsId

The workflow looks for the new worker 'id' in the response under:
  response.id | response.workerId | response.reference | response[0].id | response[0].reference

Corresponds to: POST /organizations/{organizationId}/workspaces/{workspaceId}/workers/import (operationId: ImportWorker)

item.name: Auth (reference)
item.description: Auth endpoints for reference — not directly called by the CI workflows (the workflows use a pre-existing token stored in secrets), but useful for manual testing.

request.description:
Not used directly by the CI workflows. Use this to obtain a Bearer token for manual testing.

Copy the token from the response and paste it into the collection variable 'token'.

Corresponds to: POST /auth/login (operationId: Login)

Not used by the CI workflows. Use this to refresh an expired Bearer token during manual testing.

Corresponds to: POST /auth/refresh (operationId: RefreshToken)
